Items.Title: 为什么在网站中使用 HTML？ | WHY USE HTML IN A WEBSITES ?
Items.Description:
辛格

发表于 4 月 7 日

它提供了一种描述网页结构和呈现方式的方法，包括文本、图像、链接和其他媒体。

结构：HTML 提供了一种将 Web 内容组织成逻辑部分的方法，例如标题、段落、列表和表格。 这使搜索引擎更容易索引和理解页面内容。

可访问性：HTML 为创建可供残障人士使用的可访问网站提供了基础。 通过使用语义 HTML，开发人员可以确保内容被正确标记和组织，使屏幕阅读器和其他辅助技术更容易解读页面。

调整：HTML 为创建网页提供了一套标准的元素和属性。 这确保页面在不同的浏览器和设备上保持一致，并且可以随着时间的推移轻松维护和更新。

通过使用语义 HTML 并优化页面标题、描述和其他元数据，开发人员可以提高其站点在搜索引擎结果中的可见性。

协调：HTML 可以与其他 Web 技术集成，例如用于样式的 CSS 和用于交互的 JavaScript。 这允许开发人员创建丰富的、动态的 Web 应用程序，这些应用程序可以提供更具吸引力的用户体验。

一旦暂停，theprithwisingh 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

解除暂停后，theprithwisingh 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，theprithwisingh 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 theprithwisingh 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 theprithwisingh 的方法：

降低 theprithwisingh 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 theprithwisingh 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Prithwi singh

Posted on Apr 7

HTML (Hypertext Markup Language) is the standard markup language used to create and structure content on the web. It provides a way to describe the structure and presentation of web pages, including text, images, links, and other media.

Structure: HTML provides a way to structure web content into logical sections, such as headings, paragraphs, lists, and tables. This makes it easier for search engines to index and understand the content of the page.

Accessibility: HTML provides the foundation for creating accessible websites that can be used by people with disabilities. By using semantic HTML, developers can ensure that the content is properly labeled and organized, making it easier for screen readers and other assistive technologies to interpret the page.

Adjustment: HTML provides a standard set of elements and attributes for creating web pages. This ensures that pages are consistent across different browsers and devices, and that they can be easily maintained and updated over time.

SEO: HTML provides the structure and metadata that search engines use to index web pages. By using semantic HTML and optimizing the page title, description, and other metadata, developers can improve the visibility of their site in search engine results.

Co-ordination: HTML can be integrated with other web technologies, such as that CSS for styling and JavaScript for interactivity. This allows developers to create rich, dynamic web applications that can more engaging user experience.

Once suspended, theprithwisingh will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, theprithwisingh will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by theprithwisingh will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If theprithwisingh is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag theprithwisingh:

Make all posts by theprithwisingh less visible

theprithwisingh consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging theprithwisingh will restore default visibility to their posts.
Items.Author: theprithwisingh

Items.Title: 回收码：CNAME & 重新请求 | Recycle Code: CNAME & Re-request
Items.Description:
本周，我在Starchart项目上做了一些工作，我重用了现有代码来完成我的任务。 当我发现如何利用现有代码来完成任务时，这总是一种美妙的感觉。

没有重复的 CNAME！

在一个证书下，用户可以拥有多个DNS Records 。
按照设计，CNAME DNS 记录会将您从一个域转发到另一个域，因此让两个 CNAME DNS 记录同时将您从一个域转发到另外两个域毫无意义。

因此，我们需要防止用户创建此类重复的 CNAME 记录。

一个小限制

我添加了一段代码来实现这一点：

 if (type === 'CNAME') { count = await prisma.dnsRecord.count({ where: { username, type, subdomain, }, });

该项目已经有一些代码来检查名称、类型和值相同的重复 DNS 记录。 名称是子域名。 值可能是 IP 地址或域之类的东西，具体取决于 DNS 记录的类型。

我添加的这段代码位于现有代码中，用于检查重复的 DNS 记录，以更好地适应项目并重新利用其他现有代码。 通过计算属于同一用户和具有相同子域的 CNAME 记录的数量，这增加了检查重复 DNS 记录的另一个方法。 选择这两个条件是因为用户名和子域名用于创建 CNAME 记录将从中重定向用户的域名。
回想一下，我们不想从一个域重定向到多个域。 这就是我们检查具有相同用户和子域的 CNAME 记录的原因。

它利用 Prisma 的count在数据库中进行计数。 重新申请证书

说到证书，我还和队友一起做了一个PR和peer programming ，以增加用户在即将到期时重新申请新证书的能力。

我们做了什么

该代码检查证书的validTo日期是否距现在不到 30 天。 如果是这样，重新请求按钮将在证书页面上可用，以允许用户请求另一个证书。
因为我们已经有了允许用户请求他们的第一个证书的现有代码，所以我们只需为按钮再次使用/触发该代码。

按钮的代码片段：

 <Flex justifyContent="flex-end"> <Form method="post" onSubmit={() => setIsDisabled(true)}> <IconButton type="submit" aria-label="renew-certificate" icon={<RepeatIcon />} backgroundColor="transparent" color="black" _hover={{ backgroundColor: 'brand.500', color: 'white' }} isDisabled={isDisabled} /> </Form> </Flex>

因为证书请求代码位于使用remix.js构建的操作函数下，并且它是由提交触发的，所以我们只需让这个新按钮提交以重用该代码。

我们确实发现了一个小错误，该程序只识别用户的第二个最新证书，因为代码按validTo日期降序对证书进行排序，以获取最新的证书，但真正的新证书不会有该字段短期内填写。 这导致新证书被放在排序的底部，而不是被选为当前证书。

对等编程

与另一个开发人员坐在一起编写代码很有趣。 您可以获得两种不同的观点，并且可以互相学习，无论是工具和语言方面的专业知识、编码风格还是新想法。

因为我不熟悉项目的前端，所以与熟悉该领域的另一位开发人员坐在一起非常有帮助。
他向我介绍了我们想要处理的前端代码区域是如何构建的，我们将我们的想法放在一起找出解决问题的方法。



原文:

This week, I did some work on the Starchart project where I reuse existing codes to accomplish my tasks. It's always a neat feeling when I find out how I can leverage existing code to achieve a task.

No Duplicate CNAME!

The Starchart project allows user to easily create SSL certificate by taking care of a lot of required steps. Under a certificate, users can have multiple DNS Records.
By design, CNAME DNS Records forward you from one domain to another, so it makes little sense to have two CNAME DNS Records forwarding you from one same domain to two other domains at the same time.

Because of this, we need to prevent users from creating these kind of duplicate CNAME records.

A Small Restriction

I added a piece of code to achieve that:

  if (type === 'CNAME') {
    count = await prisma.dnsRecord.count({
      where: {
        username,
        type,
        subdomain,
      },
    });

The project already has some code to check for duplicate DNS record that are the same with the name, type, and value. Name is the subdomain name. Type is the type of record, such as CNAME. Value would be things like IP address or domain, depending on the type of DNS record.

This piece of code I added sits in the existing code to check for duplicate DNS record, to fit the project better and reutilize other existing code. This adds another to checking for duplicate DNS record, by counting number of CNAME records that belong to the same user and has same subdomain. These two criteria are chosen because username and subdomain name are used to create the domain name where a CNAME record would redirect people from.
Recall that we don't want to redirect from one domain to multiple domain. This is why we check for CNAME records with same user and subdomain.

It utilizes Prisma's count to do the counting in the database. where specifies what criteria for a row to count.

Re-request Certificate

Speaking of certificate, I also did a PR with a teammate together with peer programming, to add the ability for users to re-request a new certificate when it's about to expire.

What we did

The code checks for whether a certificate's validTo date is less than 30 days from present. If so, a re-request button will become available on the certificate page to allow the user to request another certificate.
Because we already have existing code to allow users to request their first certificate, we simply use/trigger that code again for the button.

Code Snippet for the button:

              <Flex justifyContent="flex-end">
                <Form method="post" onSubmit={() => setIsDisabled(true)}>
                  <IconButton
                    type="submit"
                    aria-label="renew-certificate"
                    icon={<RepeatIcon />}
                    backgroundColor="transparent"
                    color="black"
                    _hover={{ backgroundColor: 'brand.500', color: 'white' }}
                    isDisabled={isDisabled}
                  />
                </Form>
              </Flex>

Because the certificate request code is under an action function build with remix.js, and it's triggered by submit, we simply make this new button submit to reuse that code.

We did found out a minor bug where the program was only recognizing the second newest certificate for a user, because the code was sorting certificates by validTo date in descending order, to fetch the newest one, but a really new one would not have that field filled out for a short period. This caused the new certificate to be placed at the bottom of the sorting and not be select as the current certificate.

Peer programming

It's interesting sitting with another developer to work on code together. You get tow different perspectives, and you can learn form each other, whether it's expertise with tools and languages, coding style, or new ideas.

Because I'm unfamiliar with the front-end of the project, it was very helpful to sit with another developer who is familiar with that area.
He walked me through how the area of the front-end code we wanted to work on is constructed, and we put our ideas together to figure out how to tackle the issue.
